matterName: Bayfront Steel & Supply, LLC v. Coastal Marine Constructors, Inc. (Port Verano Berth 7 Reconstruction)
practiceArea: Construction Litigation — Construction Lien & Collection (Public Project / Payment Bond)
summary: Subcontractor/supplier Bayfront Steel & Supply, LLC seeks unpaid amounts for structural steel and rebar furnished to general contractor Coastal Marine Constructors, Inc. on the Port Verano Authority's Berth 7 reconstruction. Because the project sits on land owned by a public port authority, the claim implicates whether a Chapter 713 construction lien can attach and whether recovery must instead proceed against the Section 255.05 public payment bond.
attorneyNotesMarkdown:
# Matter Notes — Bayfront Steel & Supply, LLC

**Client:** Bayfront Steel & Supply, LLC (subcontractor/supplier of structural steel + rebar)
**Adverse:** Coastal Marine Constructors, Inc. (GC) / Port Verano Authority (owner)
**Project:** Berth 7 Reconstruction, Port Verano, Hillsborough County, FL

## Posture
- We represent the supplier. Subcontract value $487,500. We have a balance dispute.
- **CRITICAL OPEN ISSUE:** The Berth 7 parcel is owned by the **Port Verano Authority**, a public port authority. A standard Chapter 713 construction lien very likely **cannot attach to public/sovereign property** — see *Deen v. Tampa Port Authority*. If true, our real remedy is the **Section 255.05 payment bond**, not the recorded lien. Confirm whether GC actually furnished a 255.05 bond.
- *Hidden fact (strategy):* Our paralegal believes the GC's 255.05 payment bond may be **defective / never recorded** with the Authority — if so, the lien-vs-bond question becomes existential for recovery. Need to pull the bond from the Authority's records.

## Numbers — WATCH THE CONTRADICTION
- Demand letter states balance due of **$182,340.00**.
- BUT our own internal draw ledger nets to **$163,815.00** unpaid. The demand letter overstates by **$18,525.00** — looks like it double-counted the disputed back-charge for the rejected Lot 4 rebar. **Fix before filing; do not repeat the $182,340 figure in the complaint.**

## Deadlines / facts to verify
- Notice to Owner served early; Claim of Lien recorded **March 11, 2026**, O.R. Book 28714, Page 1902, Hillsborough County. Lien amount on the recorded instrument is **$163,815.00** (matches the ledger, NOT the demand letter).
- *Hidden fact (buried clause):* Subcontract § 14.3 contains a **"pay-when-paid" / conditional payment** provision and a **45-day cure** requirement before suit. Confirm we satisfied the cure notice.
- One-year action deadline on the lien runs from recording.

## To do
- Decide lien vs. bond track. Re-run damages off the ledger ($163,815.00).

documents.bodyText:
STRUCTURAL STEEL & REBAR SUPPLY SUBCONTRACT
Subcontract No. CMC-BSS-2025-0047

This Subcontract is entered into as of August 4, 2025, between COASTAL MARINE CONSTRUCTORS, INC. ("Contractor"), as general contractor for the Berth 7 Reconstruction Project, and BAYFRONT STEEL & SUPPLY, LLC ("Subcontractor"), a Florida limited liability company.

1. PROJECT. The Project is the reconstruction of Berth 7 at Port Verano, located in Hillsborough County, Florida. The real property on which the Project is constructed is owned by the PORT VERANO AUTHORITY, a public port authority and political subdivision of the State of Florida ("Owner").

2. SCOPE OF WORK. Subcontractor shall furnish and deliver all structural steel, reinforcing steel (rebar), and embedded steel assemblies per Drawings S-101 through S-118, including fabrication, delivery, and unloading at the Berth 7 laydown yard.

3. CONTRACT SUM. The total Contract Sum is FOUR HUNDRED EIGHTY-SEVEN THOUSAND FIVE HUNDRED AND 00/100 DOLLARS ($487,500.00), subject to additions and deductions by approved change order.

4. PAYMENT. Contractor shall pay Subcontractor monthly progress payments based on delivered and accepted materials, less ten percent (10%) retainage.

14.3 CONDITIONAL PAYMENT; CURE. Contractor's receipt of payment from the Owner for Subcontractor's materials is a condition precedent to Contractor's obligation to pay Subcontractor (pay-when-paid). Notwithstanding any dispute, before commencing any action, the claiming party shall deliver written notice of default and afford the other party forty-five (45) days to cure.

23. PUBLIC PROJECT BOND. The Project being constructed on public property, Contractor has furnished a payment bond pursuant to Section 255.05, Florida Statutes, identified as Bond No. SUR-7741-FL issued by GULFSTREAM SURETY COMPANY. Claims for unpaid labor and materials shall be made against said bond as provided by law.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the parties have executed this Subcontract as of the date first written above.

COASTAL MARINE CONSTRUCTORS, INC.    BAYFRONT STEEL & SUPPLY, LLC
By: Raymond T. Holcomb, President        By: Dolores A. Vance, Managing Member

documents.bodyText:
LINWOOD & PRATT, P.A.
Attorneys at Law
400 N. Ashley Drive, Suite 2600, Tampa, FL 33602

March 24, 2026

VIA CERTIFIED MAIL & EMAIL
Mr. Raymond T. Holcomb, President
Coastal Marine Constructors, Inc.
1750 Causeway Blvd.
Tampa, FL 33619

RE: DEMAND FOR PAYMENT — Berth 7 Reconstruction, Port Verano
    Subcontract No. CMC-BSS-2025-0047

Dear Mr. Holcomb:

This firm represents Bayfront Steel & Supply, LLC ("Bayfront") in connection with structural steel and reinforcing steel furnished to Coastal Marine Constructors, Inc. for the above-referenced Project. Despite repeated requests, Coastal Marine has failed to pay Bayfront for materials delivered and accepted at the Berth 7 site.

DEMAND IS HEREBY MADE for payment of the outstanding balance of ONE HUNDRED EIGHTY-TWO THOUSAND THREE HUNDRED FORTY AND 00/100 DOLLARS ($182,340.00), representing unpaid invoices, released retainage, and freight charges for materials furnished through February 2026.

Bayfront has timely served its Notice to Owner and recorded its Claim of Lien. Because the Project is located on property of the Port Verano Authority, Bayfront further reserves all rights to proceed against the payment bond furnished pursuant to Section 255.05, Florida Statutes.

Unless full payment of $182,340.00 is received within FIFTEEN (15) DAYS of the date of this letter, Bayfront will commence suit to enforce its claim, together with interest, attorneys' fees, and costs as permitted by law.

Govern yourself accordingly.

Very truly yours,
Margaret E. Linwood, Esq.
Linwood & Pratt, P.A.
Counsel for Bayfront Steel & Supply, LLC

[SCANNED — RECORDED INSTRUMENT]

This instrument prepared by and return to:
Linwood & Pratt, P.A., 400 N. Ashley Drive, Suite 2600, Tampa, FL 33602

INSTR # 2026000091847
OR BK 28714  PG 1902
RECORDED 03/11/2026  HILLSBOROUGH COUNTY, FL
CINDY STUART, CLERK OF COURT

CLAIM OF LIEN
(Section 713.08, Florida Statutes)

Before me, the undersigned notary, personally appeared Dolores A. Vance, as Managing Member of BAYFRONT STEEL & SUPPLY, LLC ("Lienor"), who, being duly sworn, states:

1. Lienor furnished structural steel and reinforcing steel materials for the improvement of the following described real property in Hillsborough County, Florida: Berth 7 Reconstruction, Port Verano (Parcel No. 18-29-44-0000-Berth7).

2. The materials were furnished under a contract with COASTAL MARINE CONSTRUCTORS, INC.

3. Lienor first furnished materials on September 12, 2025, and last furnished materials on February 26, 2026.

4. There remains unpaid to Lienor the sum of ONE HUNDRED SIXTY-THREE THOUSAND EIGHT HUNDRED FIFTEEN AND 00/100 DOLLARS ($163,815.00), for which Lienor claims a lien on the above-described real property and improvements.

DOLORES A. VANCE, Managing Member
Bayfront Steel & Supply, LLC

Sworn to and subscribed before me this 9th day of March, 2026.
[NOTARY SEAL]

email.subject: RE: Berth 7 — outstanding balance / Lot 4
email.body:
Dolores,

Following up on your call. Two issues on the balance you're claiming:

1) Pay-when-paid. Under Section 14.3 of CMC-BSS-2025-0047, our payment to you is conditioned on Coastal first receiving payment from the Port Verano Authority. The Authority has NOT released our pay application covering the Lot 4 materials, so that portion is not yet due to you under the subcontract.

2) Lot 4 back-charge. We are withholding $18,525.00 against the Lot 4 rebar coils that QC flagged. Until that inspection clears, that amount stays as a back-charge.

I see you've recorded a lien. As you know, this is the Authority's property, so I'm not sure a lien gets you anywhere here — the right path is the 255.05 bond. I'm attaching the Notice to Owner you served back in December for the file.

Brian Ostrander
Controller, Coastal Marine Constructors, Inc.

--- Original Message ---
From: Dolores Vance
Sent: March 4, 2026
To: Brian Ostrander
Subject: Berth 7 — outstanding balance / Lot 4

Brian — We still show a large unpaid balance on Berth 7. INV-2026-0214 ($98,400) is fully unpaid and INV-2026-0228 hasn't been touched. The Lot 4 material was delivered and signed for on January 19. Please advise when we'll be paid. — Dolores
email.attachmentBody:
NOTICE TO OWNER
(Section 713.06 / 255.05, Florida Statutes)

Date served: December 30, 2025

TO OWNER: Port Verano Authority, 1101 Channelside Drive, Tampa, FL 33602
TO CONTRACTOR: Coastal Marine Constructors, Inc.

The undersigned hereby notifies you that BAYFRONT STEEL & SUPPLY, LLC has furnished, or is furnishing, structural steel and reinforcing steel materials for the improvement of the real property identified as Berth 7 Reconstruction, Port Verano, under an order given by Coastal Marine Constructors, Inc.

The Project being on public property, the surety payment bond on this Project is Bond No. SUR-7741-FL, GULFSTREAM SURETY COMPANY, principal Coastal Marine Constructors, Inc.

Florida law prescribes the serving of this notice and restricts your right to make payments under your contract in accordance with Section 713.06, Florida Statutes.

BAYFRONT STEEL & SUPPLY, LLC, by Dolores A. Vance, Managing Member.

documents.purpose: Born-digital internal research memo framing the public-property lien-vs-bond question and the strict-compliance issue; supports the cross-document synthesis question and the CourtListener legal issue.
hiddenFacts: Memo concludes a Chapter 713 lien likely cannot attach to Port Verano Authority's public property
Recommends pursuing the Section 255.05 payment bond (Bond No. SUR-7741-FL) as primary remedy
Notes the one-year deadline to enforce runs from the March 11, 2026 recording date
Flags that any complaint should plead $163,815.00 per the ledger, not the $182,340.00 in the demand letter
documents.bodyText:
PRIVILEGED & CONFIDENTIAL — ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT

MEMORANDUM
TO: File — Bayfront Steel & Supply (Berth 7 / Port Verano)
FROM: Margaret E. Linwood, Esq.
DATE: April 2, 2026
RE: Lien vs. Section 255.05 bond on a public port project

ISSUE. May Bayfront enforce its recorded Chapter 713 Claim of Lien against the Berth 7 parcel, or must it instead recover against the Section 255.05 payment bond, where the parcel is owned by the Port Verano Authority, a public port authority?

SHORT ANSWER. The lien almost certainly cannot attach. Florida construction liens under Chapter 713 do not attach to public or sovereign property; for improvements to public projects the lienor's remedy is a claim against the statutory payment bond under Section 255.05. See Deen v. Tampa Port Authority. Our primary track should be the 255.05 bond (Bond No. SUR-7741-FL, Gulfstream Surety), with the recorded lien preserved only as a precaution.

DISCUSSION. (1) Public property is generally immune from construction liens; the Legislature substituted the public-project payment bond as the materialman's security. (2) Construction-lien and bond-claim rights are statutory and require strict compliance — timely Notice to Owner (served December 30, 2025) and timely notice of nonpayment must be confirmed. (3) The Claim of Lien was recorded March 11, 2026 (O.R. Book 28714, Page 1902); any action to enforce must be commenced within one year of recording.

DAMAGES NOTE. Plead the balance supported by the draw ledger — $163,815.00. Do NOT repeat the $182,340.00 figure used in the March 24, 2026 demand letter; that number appears to double-count the disputed $18,525.00 Lot 4 back-charge.

RECOMMENDATION. Pursue the 255.05 bond claim; confirm the bond was properly furnished and recorded with the Authority before relying on it.

qa.question: What is the total Contract Sum under the steel and rebar subcontract, and what is the subcontract number?
What does Section 14.3 of the subcontract require before a party may file suit, and what type of payment provision does it contain?
qa.expectedAnswer: It contains a pay-when-paid conditional-payment provision (the contractor's receipt of payment from the Owner is a condition precedent) and requires written notice of default with a 45-day opportunity to cure before any action is commenced.

qa.question: What net unpaid balance does Bayfront's draw ledger actually compute to?
qa.locatorHint: Draw Ledger sheet, final 'NET UNPAID BALANCE' row

qa.question: What dollar amount does the demand letter state is owed, and how does it conflict with the draw ledger?
qa.expectedAnswer: The demand letter demands $182,340.00, but the draw ledger nets to $163,815.00 — an $18,525.00 overstatement that appears to double-count the disputed Lot 4 rebar back-charge. This is the deliberate cross-document contradiction.
qa.locatorHint: Demand paragraph ('$182,340.00') vs. ledger NET UNPAID BALANCE

qa.question: On what date was the Claim of Lien recorded, and in what Official Records book and page?
qa.expectedAnswer: Recorded March 11, 2026, in Official Records Book 28714, Page 1902, Hillsborough County (Instrument No. 2026000091847).
qa.locatorHint: Recording stamp at top of scanned instrument

qa.question: What lien amount is stated on the recorded Claim of Lien, and does it match the ledger or the demand letter?
qa.expectedAnswer: $163,815.00, which matches the draw ledger's net unpaid balance, not the demand letter's $182,340.00.
qa.locatorHint: Paragraph 4 of the Claim of Lien ('$163,815.00')

qa.question: Who signed for the Lot 4 delivery, on what date, and under what delivery ticket number?
qa.expectedAnswer: Curtis Mealy, site superintendent for Coastal Marine Constructors, signed for it on January 19, 2026 under Delivery Ticket No. DT-Lot4-0119.
qa.locatorHint: Receiving block and ticket number on the photographed ticket

qa.question: What two defenses to payment does Coastal Marine's controller raise in the email thread, and what is the payment bond identified in the attachment?
qa.expectedAnswer: He raises (1) pay-when-paid under Section 14.3 (the Authority has not released the Lot 4 pay application) and (2) an $18,525.00 back-charge for the QC-flagged Lot 4 rebar coils. The attached Notice to Owner identifies Bond No. SUR-7741-FL from Gulfstream Surety Company.
qa.locatorHint: Body items 1 and 2; attachment 'notice-to-owner.pdf' bond reference

qa.question: Given that the Berth 7 parcel is owned by the Port Verano Authority, what is the firm's recommended primary remedy and why?
qa.expectedAnswer: Pursue the Section 255.05 payment bond (Bond No. SUR-7741-FL, Gulfstream Surety) as the primary remedy, because a Chapter 713 construction lien cannot attach to public/sovereign property; the recorded lien is preserved only as a precaution. This synthesizes the subcontract's public-owner fact, the bond identified in the NTO attachment, and the research memo's conclusion.

qa.question: By when must any action to enforce the recorded lien be commenced, per the research memo?
qa.expectedAnswer: Within one year of the March 11, 2026 recording date.
qa.locatorHint: DISCUSSION point (3) referencing the one-year deadline from recording

qa.question: What is the penal sum (maximum coverage) of the Section 255.05 payment bond?
qa.expectedAnswer: NOT SUPPORTED — the documents do not answer this. The bond number and surety are identified, but no penal sum / bond amount appears anywhere in the corpus.
qa.locatorHint: Bond is named (SUR-7741-FL) but no dollar penal sum is stated in any document

chronology.event: Bayfront and Coastal Marine execute Subcontract No. CMC-BSS-2025-0047 for Berth 7 steel/rebar ($487,500.00).
Bayfront first furnishes materials to the Berth 7 project (per recorded Claim of Lien).
Bayfront serves its Notice to Owner on the Port Verano Authority, identifying Bond No. SUR-7741-FL.
Lot 4 structural steel and rebar delivered and signed for by superintendent Curtis Mealy (Ticket DT-Lot4-0119).
Bayfront last furnishes materials (final delivery + freight, INV-2026-0228).
Coastal Marine's controller asserts pay-when-paid and an $18,525.00 Lot 4 back-charge in the email dispute thread.
Claim of Lien recorded in O.R. Book 28714, Page 1902, Hillsborough County (lien amount $163,815.00).
Demand letter sent demanding $182,340.00 (overstated vs. ledger).

courtListener.legalIssue: Whether a subcontractor/supplier's Chapter 713 construction lien can attach to land owned by a public port authority, or whether its exclusive remedy for unpaid materials on a public project is a claim against the Section 255.05, Florida Statutes payment bond; and the strict-compliance requirements (Notice to Owner) governing such claims.
courtListener.jurisdiction: Florida (Supreme Court of Florida and District Courts of Appeal)
expectedAuthorityNames: Deen v. Tampa Port Authority, 207 So. 2d 688 (Fla. 1967)
City of Gainesville v. Republic Inv. Corp., 480 So. 2d 1344 (Fla. 1st DCA 1985)
Fla. Stat. ch. 713 (Construction Lien Law); § 713.08 (Claim of Lien)
